steps.text: A figure in Academy robes stands ankle-deep in the water, turning in a slow circle, muttering directions to herself and getting them wrong every time.

help.text: "The causeway? Oh—thank the Archmage, I've been trying to triangulate it from moss growth for an hour and I don't even know if that works."
She follows close behind, narrating every landmark you pass in case she needs to find it again on her own. At the causeway, she presses something into your hand before hurrying up toward the gate.

point.text: "That way? Are you—thank you!" She splashes off in the direction you indicated. You don't check if it was the right one.

leave.text: You leave her to her moss theory and keep moving. Her muttering fades behind you into the fog.

steps.text: What you took for a cluster of dead trees shifts, slowly, and you realize it has been watching you the entire time you've been standing here.

wait.text: Nothing about it hurries. After a long stretch of silence, the great shape loses interest and settles back into stillness, indistinguishable from the trees around it once more.

steps.text: You skirt around its roots without a sound. It doesn't stir.
Your foot sinks into a root you hadn't seen. The Ent turns, unhurried but entirely awake now.

on_victory.text: The great shape topples slowly, sinking back into the mud and the fog.

attack.text: You strike before it can react.

on_victory.text: The great shape topples slowly, sinking back into the mud and the fog.

steps.text: Voices drift through the fog—too soft to make out words, but close, as if just past your shoulder.

ignore.text: You push on without listening. The whispering falls behind, swallowed by the mist.

steps.text: You strain to catch the words, but they dissolve the moment you focus on them, leaving only the sound of your own breathing.
For a moment, one voice separates from the rest—a name, spoken once, gently. Not yours. Then it's gone, and the fog is only fog again.
The whispering sharpens into a wordless wail as pale shapes coalesce out of the mist around you.

on_victory.text: The shapes unravel back into the fog, and the whispering fades with them.

steps.text: Something uncoils from the reeds ahead—too many shapes, moving independently of each other. A single wide eye fixes on you through the fog.
"Yesss... another one, come to bother my watersss."

choices.choice: Avert your eyes and back away

steps.text: You keep your gaze down and retreat, step by careful step, until the reeds close between you.
A root catches your heel. The Gorgon's eye snaps toward the sound.

on_victory.text: The coiling shapes finally go still, sinking back beneath the water.

speak.text: "Sspeak? To me?" The eye narrows, considering you the way you'd consider an insect that had just done something mildly interesting.
"These watersss are mine. Walk on, and don't let me sssee you again."
You back away slowly. It doesn't follow.

attack.text: You don't wait for it to finish talking.

on_victory.text: The coiling shapes finally go still, sinking back beneath the water.

steps.text: Something pale juts from the mud ahead—not driftwood.
A carved stone face, worn almost featureless, stares up out of the muck. Whoever shaped it has been gone a very long time. You leave it where it lies.
The shape you took for stone shifts—just once—and sinks below the surface before you can see what it was.
Half-buried in the silt beside it, something catches the light.
